query: postgresql index types btree gin gist performance

rationale.https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/: PostgreSQL official documentation, Index Types (v18 current) — THE authoritative reference covering all index types: B-tree, Hash, GiST, SP-GiST, GIN, BRIN, bloom. Primary source, no substitute.
PostgreSQL official GIN documentation — detailed GIN index internals, directly on-topic for the GIN component of the query. Authoritative.
rationale.https://doi.org/10.20944/preprints202511: 'Indexing in PostgreSQL: Performance Evaluation and Use Cases' — comprehensive benchmark-based comparison of all 6 native index types across OLTP, OLAP, full-text, JSONB, spatial, time-series workloads. Produces a recommendation matrix. Exactly what the query asks for.
rationale.https://explain.technical.li/postgres-in: 'Postgres Indexes Beyond B-Tree—GIN, GiST, BRIN, SP-GiST' with real queries and 20-1000x performance claims. Practical tutorial covering all non-default index types with examples.
rationale.https://blog.devops.dev/how-to-choose-be: 'How to Choose Between Index Types (B-tree, GIN, GiST)' — decision-guide format, directly answering when to choose each type. High relevance to query framing.
rationale.https://habr.com/en/companies/postgrespr: PostgresPro (PostgreSQL core contributors) article on GIN indexes — part 7 of their deep-dive series on PostgreSQL indexing. Source from core PG team gives technical depth.
rationale.https://postgrespro.com/docs/postgresql/: PostgresPro index-types documentation (PG 14) — mirrors official docs with same B-tree/GiST/GIN/BRIN coverage. Lower priority than official PG 18 current docs but still authoritative.
rationale.https://www.meerako.com/blogs/postgresql: 'PostgreSQL Indexing Deep Dive: B-Tree, GIN, GiST' — practical strategy guide from DB experts covering the three most common types named in the query.
rationale.https://doi.org/10.1088/1742-6596/944/1/: 'Improving generalized inverted index lock wait times' — technical paper on GIN locking protocol in PostgreSQL's MVCC. Addresses concurrent GIN operations — relevant for the 'performance' dimension of the query.
rationale.https://www.thenile.dev/docs/extensions/: Nile docs on btree_gin extension — covers the hybrid use case where GIN indexes handle B-tree types. Addresses cross-type performance optimization.

notes: Pool of 23 has clear signal/noise separation. Signal: official PG docs, PostgresPro, practical tutorials, one comprehensive benchmark paper. Noise: 'Performance of the Bispectral Index During Electrocautery' (medical paper, false positive on 'index' and 'performance'), 'The Gist of Reading' book index (false positive on 'GiST'), 3 chapters from a PostgreSQL Configuration book about installation/logging (not indexing), MongoDB vs PostgreSQL broad comparison, CityGML/cjdb paper. Excluded the PostgreSQL performance optimization thesis (openalex W2612672839) as too broad — focuses on general optimization not index types specifically.
